Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts And Genuineness Of Documents And Imposing Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-13-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF JINA PARK's, ON BEHALF OF HERSELF, Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts And Genuineness Of Documents And Imposing Monetary Sanctions.

Appearances:
Nicolas Jupillat, Esq., (via Zoom videoconference) of ILG Legal Office, P.C., (415) 580-2574, for Plaintiff.
Justin P. Fong, M.D., (in person), in propria persona, Defendant.

The matter is not argued/contested. The court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff was employed by the defendant physician as an office worker for a number of months, after having volunteered at the doctor's office previously. Among other causes of action, plaintiff alleges that during the course of her employment, she was treated in a discriminatory manner due to her national origin. The defendant is appearing in propria persona. On March 29, 2024 and then again on April 23, plaintiff served a Request for Admissions seeking admissions of various factual assertions and the genuineness of an attached document, defendant did not provide timely responses. After a letter demanding responses was sent by plaintiff, and the instant motion was filed, on July 30, 2024 defendant submitted responses containing a somewhat defective verification. Although plaintiff had also served other written discovery on defendant to which responses were also late, she has evidently chosen not to address those responses in her motion. As plaintiff notes in her reply memorandum, defendant's defectively verified (and late) responses to the Request for Admissions could be viewed as tantamount to no responses at all. That said, defendant is laboring without an attorney (he certainly could use one) and if plaintiff's motion were granted, the deemed admissions would almost certainly establish liability on defendant's part. Defendant's late responses, however, suggest that his liability is at least questionable at this point, including whether jurisdiction under the Fair Employment and Housing Act, Government Code sections 12940, et seq., is warranted on the facts of this case. Therefore, "in the interests of justice", the Court is disinclined to grant plaintiff's motion at this point in time. Instead, the Court ORDERS defendant to serve upon plaintiff a proper verification of his responses to discovery (see defendant's July 30, 2024 response and exhibits thereto) by August 19, 2024. Plaintiff can continue to prosecute her claims going forward and return to defendant's alleged liability therefore at some later date. For those reasons, plaintiff's motion is DENIED, albeit without prejudice. However, defendant's handling of plaintiff's discovery efforts resulted in plaintiff having to prepare and file the instant motion when all that could have been avoided with timely verified responses. Accordingly, plaintiff's request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $2,685, payable no later than August 30, 2024.

Plaintiff's counsel is directed to prepare a form of proposed order consistent with the foregoing and submit it to psw@hassard.com for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (525/JPT)
